title: Cost of Living by State 2026: Affordability Ranked
description: Discover the complete 2026 cost of living by state ranking. Compare all 50 US states by housing, groceries, effective tax burden, and true affordability.

schemas.headline: Cost of Living by State 2026
schemas.description: Discover the complete 2026 cost of living by state ranking. Compare all 50 US states by housing, groceries, effective tax burden, and true affordability.

mainEntity.name: What are the cheapest states to live in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The cheapest states typically include those in the South and Midwest, such as Oklahoma, Mississippi, and West Virginia, driven by low housing and utility costs.

mainEntity.name: Which states have the highest cost of living?

acceptedAnswer.text: Hawaii, Massachusetts, California, and New York consistently rank among the most expensive states due to severe housing shortages and high state taxes.

mainEntity.name: How is the cost of living index calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: The index compares state-level prices for housing, groceries, utilities, transportation, and healthcare to a national baseline average set at 100.

mainEntity.name: Does cost of living include taxes?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard cost of living indexes often exclude taxes. However, true affordability must factor in state income, property, and sales taxes to calculate effective take-home pay.

mainEntity.name: How does median income affect state affordability?

acceptedAnswer.text: A high cost of living can be offset by higher median incomes. Affordability is best measured by dividing post-tax effective income by the regional cost of living index.
